checks.describe: the rank sweep attaches a LoRA at r=4/8/16/32 and prints the trainable-% table without any training (section 1). Asserts the table shape, not a run-specific loss
checks.run: uv run --quiet labs/m6/rank_sweep.py 2>/dev/null | grep -Ec 'trainable %|0\.[0-9]+%' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 2 ]; then echo sweep-ok; else echo sweep-bad; fi'

checks.describe: the sweep confirms trainable params roughly double from r=8 to r=16 — the linear-in-rank point (section 1)
checks.run: OUT=$(uv run --quiet labs/m6/rank_sweep.py --ranks 8 16 2>/dev/null); R8=$(echo "$OUT" | awk '$1==8{gsub(",","",$2); print $2}'); R16=$(echo "$OUT" | awk '$1==16{gsub(",","",$2); print $2}'); if [ -n "$R8" ] && [ -n "$R16" ] && [ "$R16" -gt "$R8" ] 2>/dev/null; then echo rank-linear; else echo rank-odd; fi

checks.describe: the chat-template demo prints the model's own template (with role markers) next to a naive concatenation — the train/serve mismatch failure mode (section 2)
checks.run: uv run --quiet labs/m6/chat_template_demo.py 2>/dev/null | grep -Ec 'im_start|CORRECT|WRONG' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 2 ]; then echo template-demo-ok; else echo template-demo-bad; fi'

checks.describe: the catastrophic-forgetting probe exists and asks out-of-domain questions to compare base vs tuned (section 3)
checks.run: F=labs/m6/forgetting_probe.py; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'PROBES' "$F" && grep -q 'chat/completions' "$F"; then echo probe-present; else echo probe-missing; fi

checks.describe: the training-container Dockerfile exists with pinned deps and runs the trainer as its entrypoint (section 4)
checks.run: F=labs/m6/Dockerfile.train; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'train_lora.py' "$F" && grep -q 'torch==' "$F"; then echo dockerfile-ok; else echo dockerfile-missing; fi

checks.describe: the optional Colab QLoRA notebook exists, is valid JSON, and uses 4-bit quantization (bitsandbytes) — the GPU enrichment track (section 4)
checks.run: F=labs/m6/colab_qlora.ipynb; if [ -s "$F" ] && jq -e '.cells | length > 0' "$F" >/dev/null 2>&1 && grep -q 'load_in_4bit' "$F"; then echo colab-ok; else echo colab-bad; fi
